content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer NNN, construct two arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN such that:


AiÔëáAjA_i \ne A_jAiÔÇőţÇá=AjÔÇő for all (1ÔëĄi<jÔëĄN)(1\le i \lt j \le N)(1ÔëĄi<jÔëĄN);
BiÔëáBjB_i \ne B_jBiÔÇőţÇá=BjÔÇő for all (1ÔëĄi<jÔëĄN)(1\le i \lt j \le N)(1ÔëĄi<jÔëĄN);
AiÔëáBjA_i \ne B_jAiÔÇőţÇá=BjÔÇő for all (1ÔëĄi,jÔëĄN)(1\le i,j \le N)(1ÔëĄi,jÔëĄN);
1ÔëĄAi,BiÔëĄ3Ôőů1051 \leq A_i, B_i \leq 3\cdot 10^51ÔëĄAiÔÇő,BiÔÇőÔëĄ3Ôőů105 for all (1ÔëĄiÔëĄN)(1\leq i \leq N)(1ÔëĄiÔëĄN);
ÔŐĽA[1,i]=ÔŐĽB[1,i]\oplus A[1,i] = \oplus B[1,i]ÔŐĽA[1,i]=ÔŐĽB[1,i] for all (1ÔëĄiÔëĄN)(1\le i \le N)(1ÔëĄiÔëĄN) such that iii is **even**.
Here ÔŐĽA[L,R]\oplus A[L,R]ÔŐĽA[L,R] corresponds to the bitwise XOR of all elements of the subarray [L,R][L,R][L,R] of array AAA. Similarly, ÔŐĽB[L,R]\oplus B[L,R]ÔŐĽB[L,R] corresponds to the bitwise XOR of all elements of the subarray [L,R][L,R][L,R] of array BBB.

If multiple answers are possible, ouput any.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, a single integer NNN - as mentioned in statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output two lines.



The first line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the elements of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the elements of the array BBB.

If multiple answers are possible, ouput any.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2182^{18}218.
